message:
test(acl): grant +@all to SETUSER scaffold in core_acl fixture

Update the core_acl.json conformance fixture so the baseline
`ACL SETUSER testuser on >secret123` scaffold also grants `+@all`,
matching what the test harness actually needs for follow-on ACL
assertions.

Without `+@all`, the user is created with the Redis default-deny
command bitmap, so subsequent conformance steps that exercise
testuser's ability to run specific commands fail the precondition
check before reaching the ACL logic under test. Granting `+@all`
inline with the initial SETUSER call keeps the fixture
self-contained (no separate ACL SETUSER +@all follow-up) and
matches the upstream Redis ACL tutorial examples, which uniformly
include a category grant when scaffolding a test user.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
